subject: current-affairs
description: Afghanistan Pakistan Conflict 2025 MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: The international border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is known as the:
mcqs.explanation: The Pakistan–Afghanistan border is known as the Durand Line, running for about 2,640 km.

mcqs.q: The Durand Line was demarcated in:
mcqs.explanation: The Durand Line Agreement was signed in 1893 between British India and the Amir of Afghanistan.

mcqs.q: The Durand Line is named after:
mcqs.explanation: The line is named after Sir Mortimer Durand, then Foreign Secretary of British India, who signed the 1893 agreement.

mcqs.q: The Durand Line agreement was signed with which Afghan ruler?
mcqs.explanation: The 1893 Durand Line agreement was signed with Afghan Amir Abdur Rahman Khan.

mcqs.q: The total length of the Durand Line is approximately:
mcqs.explanation: The Durand Line runs for approximately 2,640 km, making it one of the world's most rugged international borders.

mcqs.q: Afghanistan's position on the Durand Line has historically been:
options: Full recognition as an international border
mcqs.explanation: Successive Afghan governments have refused to formally recognise the Durand Line as an international border — a long-standing source of Pak-Afghan tension.

mcqs.q: The Afghan Taliban returned to power in Afghanistan in:
mcqs.explanation: The Afghan Taliban captured Kabul on 15 August 2021 as US and NATO forces withdrew, returning to power after 20 years.

mcqs.q: The formal name of the Afghan Taliban government is:
mcqs.explanation: The Taliban call their government the 'Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan' (IEA) — the same name they used during their 1996–2001 rule.

mcqs.q: The Supreme Leader (Amir-ul-Momineen) of the Afghan Taliban is:
mcqs.explanation: Hibatullah Akhundzada has been the Taliban's Supreme Leader since the death of Mullah Akhtar Mansour in 2016; he remains so after the 2021 return to power.

mcqs.q: The Afghan Taliban's acting Foreign Minister is:
mcqs.explanation: Amir Khan Muttaqi serves as the Afghan Taliban's acting Foreign Minister.

mcqs.q: The Afghan Taliban's acting Defence Minister, son of Mullah Omar, is:
mcqs.explanation: Mullah Muhammad Yaqoob, son of Taliban founder Mullah Omar, is the acting Defence Minister in the Afghan Taliban government.

mcqs.q: The capital of Afghanistan is:
mcqs.explanation: Kabul is the capital of Afghanistan.

mcqs.q: The Afghan Taliban's de-facto power centre and the Supreme Leader's seat is based in:
mcqs.explanation: Although the formal capital is Kabul, Kandahar is where Supreme Leader Hibatullah Akhundzada is based — making it the Taliban's real power centre.

mcqs.q: TTP stands for:
mcqs.explanation: TTP stands for Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan — a Pakistani militant umbrella organisation.

mcqs.q: The Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) was formally founded in:
mcqs.explanation: The TTP was formally founded in December 2007 under its first leader, Baitullah Mehsud, uniting several Pakistani militant factions.

mcqs.q: The current leader of Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) is:
mcqs.explanation: Mufti Noor Wali Mehsud has been the leader (Ameer) of the TTP since 2018.

mcqs.q: The founder of TTP, who was killed in a US drone strike in 2009, was:
mcqs.explanation: Baitullah Mehsud, the founding Ameer of TTP, was killed in a US drone strike in South Waziristan in August 2009.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's position is that TTP attacks inside Pakistan are planned from:
mcqs.explanation: Islamabad consistently says that TTP leaders and fighters operate from Afghan soil, launching attacks into Pakistan — this is the core grievance behind the 2025 escalation.

mcqs.q: The main border crossing between Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (Pakistan) and Afghanistan is:
mcqs.explanation: Torkham is the primary crossing between KP (Pakistan) and Nangarhar (Afghanistan); the main road from Peshawar to Kabul runs through it.

mcqs.q: Torkham connects Pakistan's Khyber district to which Afghan province?
mcqs.explanation: Torkham links Pakistan's Khyber district (KP) to Nangarhar province in eastern Afghanistan.

mcqs.q: The main border crossing between Balochistan (Pakistan) and Afghanistan is:
mcqs.explanation: Chaman (opposite Spin Boldak) is the main crossing between Balochistan and Kandahar province.

mcqs.q: Chaman crossing connects Pakistan's Balochistan to which Afghan province?
mcqs.explanation: The Chaman–Spin Boldak crossing links Pakistan's Balochistan to Kandahar province in southern Afghanistan.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's comprehensive counter-terrorism operation approved in 2024 is named:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Azm-e-Istehkam was approved by the Central Apex Committee under PM Shehbaz Sharif in June 2024 as a nationwide counter-terrorism campaign.

mcqs.q: Operation Azm-e-Istehkam was launched on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Azm-e-Istehkam was launched on 22 June 2024.

mcqs.q: Pakistan launched the 'Illegal Foreigners Repatriation Plan' — primarily affecting undocumented Afghan nationals — starting in:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan launched the Illegal Foreigners Repatriation Plan in November 2023, giving undocumented foreigners a deadline to leave before deportation drives began.

mcqs.q: The single largest group affected by Pakistan's 2023 illegal-foreigners deportation drive is:
mcqs.explanation: Afghan nationals are by far the largest group affected by the Illegal Foreigners Repatriation Plan, with hundreds of thousands repatriated.

mcqs.q: The major 2025 escalation between Pakistan and the Afghan Taliban occurred in which month?
mcqs.explanation: The major cross-border escalation occurred in October 2025, with airstrikes and clashes followed by Qatar/Turkey-mediated ceasefire talks.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's airstrikes inside Afghanistan in 2025 reportedly targeted:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's airstrikes were reported to target TTP hideouts and militant camps inside Afghanistan — not Afghan civilian or government sites.

mcqs.q: During the October 2025 clashes, which major border crossing(s) were closed?
mcqs.explanation: Both Torkham and Chaman border crossings were closed during the October 2025 escalation, disrupting trade and transit.

mcqs.q: The first round of ceasefire talks between Pakistan and the Afghan Taliban in 2025 was hosted in:
mcqs.explanation: The first round of Pakistan–Afghan Taliban ceasefire talks in 2025 was hosted in Doha, Qatar.

mcqs.q: Follow-up rounds of the 2025 Pakistan–Afghan Taliban talks were held in:
mcqs.explanation: Follow-up rounds of the 2025 Pakistan–Afghan Taliban talks were held in Istanbul, hosted by Turkey.

mcqs.q: The two primary international mediators of the 2025 Pakistan–Afghan Taliban ceasefire were:
mcqs.explanation: Qatar and Turkey were the two primary international mediators of the 2025 Pakistan–Afghan Taliban ceasefire.

mcqs.q: The Prime Minister of Pakistan during the 2025 Afghanistan conflict was:
mcqs.explanation: PM Shehbaz Sharif led Pakistan's coalition government during the 2025 Afghanistan–Pakistan confrontation.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister during the 2025 conflict was:
mcqs.explanation: Ishaq Dar served concurrently as Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister during the 2025 conflict.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's Army Chief — promoted to Field Marshal just before the Afghanistan escalation — is:
mcqs.explanation: Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir has been Pakistan's Army Chief since November 2022 and was promoted to Field Marshal on 20 May 2025.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's policy demand on Afghan soil primarily focuses on:
options: Withdrawal of Afghan troops from the Durand Line
Action against TTP and other militants based in Afghanistan
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's core demand to the Afghan Taliban is sustained and verifiable action against TTP and other militant outfits operating from Afghan soil.

mcqs.q: The 2020 agreement that preceded the US withdrawal from Afghanistan was signed in:
mcqs.explanation: The US–Taliban 'Doha Agreement' was signed in Doha, Qatar in February 2020, paving the way for US withdrawal and the Taliban's return to power in August 2021.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a border crossing between Pakistan and Afghanistan?
mcqs.explanation: Wagah is the Pakistan–India border crossing near Lahore. Torkham, Chaman and Ghulam Khan are all Pakistan–Afghanistan crossings.

mcqs.q: Pakistan officially considers the Durand Line as:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's consistent official position is that the Durand Line is an internationally recognised international border.

mcqs.q: The 2025 Afghanistan–Pakistan conflict, in diplomatic terms, is best described as:
options: A brief cross-border escalation followed by a mediated ceasefire
A proxy war between two third parties
mcqs.explanation: The 2025 conflict was a brief but intense cross-border escalation — cross-border airstrikes and clashes — that was de-escalated through Qatar/Turkey-mediated talks and a ceasefire.

mcqs.q: A stable Pakistan–Afghanistan relationship is most important for which CPEC-adjacent project?
options: Trans-Afghan trade connectivity and TAPI gas pipeline
mcqs.explanation: Stable Pak-Afghan relations are critical for Trans-Afghan trade, the TAPI (Turkmenistan-Afghanistan-Pakistan-India) gas pipeline and regional connectivity projects.
